tasks.title: Add two-pass plan mode support for OpenCode runner
evidence: Claude runner supports two_pass_plan but OpenCode does not; documentation says "will expand to OpenCode in the future"
The two_pass_plan_enabled_for: false in task_cmd.rs and scan_cmd.rs comments indicate this is tracked technical debt
plan: Implement run_opencode_two_pass function similar to run_claude_two_pass
Add plan generation phase using RepoPrompt or equivalent tooling
Add implementation phase with auto-approval
Update task_cmd.rs and scan_cmd.rs to enable two_pass_plan for OpenCode
Add tests for OpenCode two-pass behavior

tasks.title: Add task priority field and sorting capabilities
evidence: Queue is ordered by priority but there is no explicit priority field
Tasks must be manually reordered by editing YAML
No way to sort by "importance" without manual reordering
plan: Add priority field to Task (enum: critical, high, medium, low)
Add queue sort --by=priority command
Add queue list --sort-by=priority flag
Update task build and scan prompts to request priority from agent
Add tests for priority sorting and validation

tasks.title: Add git conflict detection and recovery in post_run_supervise
evidence: post_run_supervise commits changes but doesn't detect merge conflicts
If git push fails due to diverged history, the state is unclear
No check for merge markers before committing
plan: Add gitutil::has_merge_conflicts() to detect conflict markers
In post_run_supervise, check for conflicts after dirty check
If conflicts found, bail with instructions to resolve
Add gitutil::is_diverged() to check if local is behind remote
Add tests for conflict detection scenarios

tasks.title: Implement per-task output directory isolation
evidence: Runners share the same working directory; simultaneous or overlapping runs could interfere
No isolation between task executions for temp files or artifacts
If run loop runs multiple tasks in parallel (future), they would conflict
plan: Add .ralph/work/<task-id> working directory per task execution
Set runner working directory to task-specific work dir
Copy/link necessary files into work dir before execution
Clean up work dir after task completes (on success or failure)
Add fsutil::create_task_workdir and cleanup helpers
Add tests for work directory lifecycle

tasks.title: Add concurrent task execution support with worker pools
evidence: run loop executes tasks sequentially; no parallel execution option
For independent tasks (no dependencies), parallel execution would be much faster
Modern runtimes have many cores; sequential execution is underutilization
plan: Add --workers/-j flag to run loop (default 1 for sequential)
Use rayon or tokio to execute N tasks concurrently
Ensure queue lock is held but tasks run in isolation (see RQ-0114)
Limit concurrency based on available CPU cores
Add tests for concurrent execution correctness

tasks.title: Add task retry mechanism with exponential backoff
evidence: Transient runner failures (network, API timeouts) cause task to fail completely
No built-in retry for flaky runners or temporary infrastructure issues
Users must manually re-run tasks that failed transiently
plan: Add retry field to Task (count, backoff_seconds)
In run_one, catch runner errors and check if retries remain
On retry, log the retry attempt and wait with backoff
Update task status to track retry_count in notes
Add tests for retry logic with mock runner failures

tasks.title: Implement task template library for recurring patterns
evidence: Common task patterns (e.g., "add tests for X", "refactor Y") are re-entered manually each time
scan and task build prompts don't have access to prior task definitions
No way to quickly create tasks from predefined templates
plan: Create .ralph/templates/ directory for task template YAML files
Add ralph task list-templates command
Add ralph task create-from-template <name> command
Templates include default tags, scope patterns, and plan structure
Add tests for template loading and instantiation

tasks.title: Implement task archiving policies (auto-archive after N days)
evidence: done.yaml grows indefinitely; no automatic cleanup
Old completed tasks accumulate forever
No way to auto-archive tasks older than X days
plan: Add archive_policy field to QueueConfig (retention_days, auto_archive)
Add queue archive --apply-policy command
Run auto-archive during queue validate or init
Add prompt before deletion (dry-run with --dry-run flag)
Add tests for archival policy calculation and execution

tasks.title: Add subtask support and hierarchical task breakdown
evidence: Large tasks must be broken down into multiple top-level tasks manually
No parent-child relationship between tasks
Cannot expand a task to see its subtasks or collapse them
plan: Add parent_id field to Task for subtasks
Add queue show --recursive to display task hierarchy
Add queue expand <task-id> to break down a task into subtasks
Add queue flatten to move subtasks to top-level
Update validation to prevent cycles in parent-child relationships
Add tests for hierarchy operations

tasks.title: Implement task scheduling (defer to future date/time)
evidence: No way to schedule a task for "next week" or "tomorrow at 9am"
All tasks are immediately actionable (unless manually blocked)
Cannot set start_date or due_date on tasks
plan: Add scheduled_for and due_by fields to Task (RFC3339 timestamps)
Update run_one to skip tasks where scheduled_for > now
Add queue list --overdue to show past-due tasks
Add queue schedule <task-id> <timestamp> command
Log warnings when approaching due date
Add tests for scheduling behavior

tasks.title: Implement task links and references
evidence: Tasks cannot reference related tasks, PRs, or issues
No way to say "this blocks RQ-0100" or "implements RQ-0099"
Cross-references would improve task discoverability
plan: Add related_tasks and blocks/is_blocked_by fields to Task
Add queue link <from-id> <to-id> command to establish relationships
Update validation to prevent self-references and enforce consistency
Add queue show --links to display relationship graph
Add tests for relationship tracking

tasks.title: Add batch task operations for bulk edits
evidence: No way to update multiple tasks at once
Cannot set all "database" tags to "backend" in one command
Bulk operations require manual YAML editing or multiple CLI calls
plan: Add queue batch set-status --filter=tag:database --to=doing command
Add queue batch set-tag --filter=tag:rust --add=performance command
Add queue batch delete --filter=status:todo --force command
Implement dry-run with --dry-run flag to preview changes
Add tests for batch operations correctness

tasks.title: Implement task diff and change tracking
evidence: No way to see what changed between task edits
Cannot audit who modified a task and when
No history of task state transitions
plan: Add .ralph/task_history.yaml or embed history in task notes
Track each state change with timestamp and "actor" (user or runner)
Add queue history <task-id> command to show transition log
Add queue diff <task-id> to show against done archive version
Add tests for history tracking and diff calculation

tasks.title: Add webhook or notification integration for task events
evidence: No external notifications when tasks complete or fail
Teams must manually check queue status
Integrations with Slack, Discord, email would improve visibility
plan: Add webhooks field to Config (URL list)
Send HTTP POST on task state changes with event payload
Add ralph queue test-webhook <url> command
Support signing webhooks with HMAC secret
Add retry logic for failed webhook deliveries
Add tests for webhook delivery and signing

tasks.title: Implement task import/export for cross-repo sharing
evidence: No way to export tasks to share with another team or repo
Cannot import tasks from external sources
Task templates or best practices cannot be easily distributed
plan: Add ralph queue export --output=tasks.yaml command
Add ralph queue import --input=tasks.yaml command
Support --filter flag to export only matching tasks
Validate imported tasks against schema and existing IDs
Add --merge flag to combine imported tasks with existing queue
Add tests for import/export correctness

tasks.title: Implement multi-repo workspace support
evidence: Ralph assumes a single repo rooted at repo_root
Monorepos with multiple projects need separate queues per subproject
No way to run tasks against different repos from one Ralph instance
plan: Add workspaces config with repo list and path mappings
Update run_cmd to execute tasks in specific repo contexts
Add --repo flag to run/scan/task commands to target repo
Support relative paths and workspace root discovery
Add tests for multi-repo task execution

tasks.title: Add signal handling for graceful shutdown during run loop
evidence: Ctrl+C during run loop may leave repo in inconsistent state
No SIGTERM/SIGINT handler for graceful shutdown
Long-running tasks cannot be cleanly interrupted mid-execution
plan: Add signal handler for SIGTERM and SIGINT in run_cmd
On interrupt, wait for current runner subprocess to complete
Set a timeout for graceful shutdown, then force kill if needed
Save partial progress before exiting
Add "Interrupted" status to Task to show incomplete runs
Add tests for graceful shutdown behavior

tasks.title: Implement task ownership and assignment
evidence: No way to assign tasks to specific users or teams
Cannot filter tasks by "who is working on what"
Team collaboration requires manual coordination
plan: Add assigned_to field to Task (string or list)
Add queue assign <task-id> <user> command
Add queue list --assigned-to=<user> filter
Update scan and task prompts to request assignment from agent
Add validation to ensure assigned users are in allowlist (if configured)
Add tests for assignment filtering

tasks.title: Add task estimation and time tracking fields
evidence: No way to record time estimates vs actual time
Cannot track "this task was estimated at 2h but took 4h"
Planning for future sprints requires historical time data
plan: Add estimate_minutes and actual_minutes fields to Task
Add queue set-time <task-id> <estimate|actual>=<minutes> command
Calculate actual from created_at to completed_at as fallback
Add queue report --estimates to show estimate vs actual accuracy
Add tests for time tracking calculations

tasks.title: Implement task aliases and shortcuts
evidence: Task IDs are numeric; users must remember "RQ-0100" format
No way to create named shortcuts like "fix-auth-bug"
Cannot reference tasks by memorable names in commands
plan: Add aliases field to Task (list of strings)
Add queue alias <task-id> <name> command
Add queue unalias <name> command
Support <alias> in any command that takes <task-id>
Validate aliases are unique and don't shadow IDs
Add tests for alias resolution and conflicts

tasks.title: Add task branching and alternative scenarios
evidence: No support for "if X fails, try Y instead" scenarios
Cannot create alternative approaches within a single task
Teams must create multiple top-level tasks for Plan A/B/C
plan: Add alternatives field to Task (list of alternative plans)
Add queue list-alternatives <task-id> to show Plan B/C options
Add queue mark-as-primary <task-id> to promote alternative to main plan
Update prompts to suggest alternatives when primary plan fails
Add tests for alternative plan management

tasks.title: Implement task rollbacks and undo functionality
evidence: No way to undo a completed task and restore previous state
If a task makes things worse, you must manually revert its changes
Cannot "rewind" to before a task ran
plan: Add ralph run undo <n> command to revert last N task commits
Use git revert with commit message matching to identify task commits
Move task from done back to queue with status=todo
Add --dry-run flag to preview what would be reverted
Add tests for undo correctness and edge cases

tasks.title: Implement task sandboxing with container support
evidence: Tasks run directly on host system with full access
No isolation for potentially dangerous operations
Cannot run tasks in Docker/podman for reproducibility
plan: Add container field to Task (image: String, volumes: Vec<String>)
Update runutil to wrap runner in container execution
Support both Docker and Podman backends
Add fallback to host execution if container unavailable
Add tests for container invocation (requires container runtime)

tasks.title: Add task dependencies on external resources
evidence: Tasks cannot specify prerequisites like "service X must be running"
No validation that required external APIs/services are available
Cannot defer task execution until external conditions are met
plan: Add requires field to Task (list of external dependencies)
Define dependency types: service, url, env_var, file_exists
Add queue check-dependencies <task-id> command
Check dependencies in run_one before executing task
Add tests for dependency checking and validation

YAML test code in stress_queue_contract_test.rs:295

plan: Create JSON schema source of truth document at docs/queue-schema.json or .ralph/schema.json
Generate JSON schema from Rust contracts using schemars to ensure consistency
Add schema export command: ralph queue schema to output the current schema

Remove YAML fallback logic from config.rs load_layer (line 82-92)
Remove YAML fallback logic from queue.rs load_queue (line 30-39)

Update prompts.rs constants to reference .json files instead of .yaml
Update init_cmd.rs to remove YAML validation fallback
Remove YAML test code from stress_queue_contract_test.rs
Update all prompt templates (worker.md, task_builder.md, scan.md) to reference JSON instead of YAML
Update AGENTS.md and .ralph/README.md to reference JSON files instead of YAML
Run make ci to ensure all changes pass
tasks.request: Now that we have switched to JSON for the queue format we need to remove all YAML code from the codebase. Users will be required to migrate or rebuild existing queue docs to the JSON format manually or via external means. This decision is final. Make sure we have a defined JSON schema doc that is our queue JSON schema source of truth and the code utilizes it. That way modifying the schema going forward is much easier.

tasks.request: Several files in this project are thousand of lines long. We need to refactor while maintaing the same functionality. Smaller file sizes are easier to maintain and find what is needed. Also easier to make sure we are not violating DRY and YAGNI principals. Create several tasks to completely tackle this effort. use the queue fields at your disposal to ensure this is done logically.

tasks.title: Add config option to control automatic git revert behavior
evidence: Current git revert behavior is automatic with no user control
gitutil::revert_uncommitted is called from runutil.rs on runner errors and from run_cmd.rs on CI failure
runutil.rs has revert_on_error bool flag but no config option
User request: 'Add a config option to control automatic the existing git revert changes behavior. I want to support Ask (default), enabled, disabled.'
plan: Add GitRevertMode enum to contracts.rs with variants: Ask (default), Enabled, Disabled
Add git_revert_mode field to AgentConfig in contracts.rs
Add merge_from support for git_revert_mode in AgentConfig
Update config.rs validate_config to handle the new option
Modify runutil.rs to respect git_revert_mode: when Ask, prompt user; when Enabled, revert; when Disabled, skip revert and exit with message
Update run_cmd.rs CI failure handling to respect git_revert_mode
Add CLI flag --git-revert-mode to override config at runtime
Update .ralph/README.md to document the new git_revert_mode config option
Add tests for all three modes
Run make ci to ensure build passes
notes: Add git revert mode config/CLI to control rollback behavior
Respect revert mode across run/scan/task flows with interactive ask handling
Add integration tests for enabled/disabled/ask non-tty CI failure behavior
Document git_revert_mode in .ralph/README.md
tasks.request: Add a config option to control automatic the existing git revert changes behavior. I want to support Ask (default), enabled, disabled. With Ask enabled by default anytime the current git revert functionality happens instead the user is prompted and asked what they'd like to do. User can then decide to revert and continue, do not revert and exit, any other options worth adding. Enabled mode maintains functionality as it currently works today. Disabled means the supervisor does NOT auto revert and instead just exits the loops leaving the repo intact with a message as to why it was exited.

tasks.title: Unify ralph task and queue command naming and behavior
evidence: User request: audit 'ralph task' and 'ralph queue' command overlap, remove confusion, and settle on a convention.
User request: change naming to prefer 'ralph task done <ID>' over 'ralph queue complete', and add 'ralph task ready <ID>' for draft->todo.
plan: Inventory current 'ralph task' and 'ralph queue' subcommands, help text, and behavior to identify overlap and conflicts.
Define the new command convention (e.g., task lifecycle commands under 'ralph task') and map old commands to new behavior, documenting any breaking changes.
Update CLI implementation and help examples to reflect the new convention while preserving 'ralph task "<request>"' behavior.
Add or update tests to cover new command names, legacy behavior (if kept), and task state transitions including done.json moves.
Run targeted CLI checks and ensure task queue transitions are correct.
tasks.request: Review commands for consistency issues for usability. Specifically 'ralph queue complete' which I think makes more sense to be 'ralph task done <ID>' which marks that task ID done and move to done.json. Same for 'ralph task ready <ID>' which marks a task in status draft as 'todo'. I want to still be able to run 'ralph task "New task description" and have new tasks be added to the queue, this functionality does not need to change. Essentially, do an audit of the 'ralph task' and 'ralph queue' commands and figure out where there is overlap and get rid of confusion by settling on a convention.

tasks.title: Align queue validation requirements with schema/docs/backfill
evidence: crates/ralph/src/queue/validation.rs enforces non-empty tags/scope/evidence/plan and requires request/created_at/updated_at via ensure_list_non_empty and ensure_field_present.
schemas/queue.schema.json only requires task id/title and treats other fields as optional/defaulted.
docs/queue-and-tasks.md lists tags/scope/evidence/plan/request/created_at/updated_at as optional fields.
plan: Decide the canonical contract for required task fields (schema/docs vs validation) and document the decision.
Update queue validation and backfill/repair logic to match the chosen contract.
Regenerate schema/docs/tests to reflect the new required/optional field set.
Ensure scan prompts, task builder prompts, etc reflect the actual required fields and schema decidied on.
Ensure prompts reflect the desired and required functionality for this task.
notes: Aligned queue validation logic to allow empty lists (tags, scope, evidence, plan) and optional request/timestamps.
Updated docs/queue-and-tasks.md to reflect optional fields.

Verified schema already supports optional fields.
Passed make ci.

validate_dependencies in crates/ralph/src/queue/validation.rs assumes depends_on IDs exist and will fail after remap unless references are updated.

tasks.title: Handle TUI event loop errors instead of panicking
evidence: crates/ralph/src/tui/mod.rs uses unwrap() for event::read, now_utc_rfc3339, and handle_key_event in the main loop, which can panic and leave the terminal in raw mode.
plan: Replace unwrap calls with error propagation and ensure terminal cleanup on failure.
Add tests or integration coverage to confirm graceful error handling.
Verify TUI exits cleanly after an injected error.
Ensure prompts reflect the desired and required functionality for this task.
notes: Replaced unwrap() with error propagation in TUI event loop
Ensured terminal cleanup happens on error via catch_unwind pattern
Handled TUI errors gracefully by logging to UI instead of crashing where possible

tasks.title: Regex search should honor --match-case

plan: Switch to RegexBuilder and set case_insensitive when match_case is false.
Add tests verifying case-insensitive regex behavior.
Update CLI help text if needed.
Ensure prompts reflect the desired and required functionality for this task.
notes: Honor --match-case for regex search via RegexBuilder
